content.full_text:
October 31, 1977


Mrs. Affa M. Hawkes
2025 North Texas Street
Fairfield, California 94533

Dear Mrs. Hawkes:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation regarding your letter of
August 29, 1977, the staff has determined that the Citizens
Committee for Action does not have a reporting obligation as a
result of the reproduction and distribution of the attached flyer.
During our phone conversation you stated that the flyer has been
distributed for posting on bulletin boards of police/fire depart-
ments, city hall, Solano College, community groups, etc. You
also stated that the flyer was strictly informational and quoted
the Elections Code. Based on this information and the fact that
there has not been any attempt to influence the action of the
voters in support or opposition of candidates or measures appearing
on the ballot in the election and there was no attempt to limit the
distribution to specific individuals, the flyer should be considered
a non-reportable activity of the committee.

You also asked what the filing deadlines are for your November 8
special election. You should check with your local filing officer
for these dates because there may be a run-off within 60 days after
the first election in which case one schedule applies. If there is
no run-off, another schedule must be used. Your local filing officer
or city attorney should be able to assist you.

Sincerely,


Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
mak

September 22, 1977


Mrs. Affa M. Hawkes
2025 North Texas Street
Fairfield, California  94533

Dear Mrs. Hawkes:

Thank you for your letter of August 29, 1977 transmitting a copy
of a flyer the Citizens Committee for Action intends to print and
disseminate to various clubs, organizations etc. You asked what
disclosure, if any, would be necessary regarding the cost of the
paper used in the printing of this flyer.

Before I can respond to your letter I will need additional infor-
mation. Please call me at your convenience so we may discuss the
matter.

Sincerely,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

Mike Baker and Ted Prim                                                 9/6/77

                                                 [illegible handwritten text]

Gina                                             [illegible handwritten text]


Attached is a letter from Alfa Hawkes concerning a piece of
literature her organization intends to circulate prior to a
special election to be held November 8, 1977. She would like
to know whether or not the circulation of this piece of material
constitutes a contribution or expenditure with the election. I
would appreciate your advice.

Thanks.


RSK/gt
Attachment

cc: Barbara, John K., Jay, John G., Alan

Her Political Practice Com
Technical Assistance and Analysis Div
900 Kay St
Sacramento, Cal 95814

Dear Miss Kutchine

Enclosed please find a fact sheet
C.C.A is planning to send to different
Clubs Organizations Corporations and
put a mailing on the over as a public
Service to the Community has the
last of papers constitute any disclaimer?

Fairfield's Mayor resigned in June1977
the Council decided on Special election
on Nov 8, 1977. What is the exact cut off
date and deadline filing date for
filing forms?
Wishing you a good year I remain
Sincerely Yours
Abe M. Hawkee

[At top of page in different handwriting:]
Enfield dated
Aug 29 1977
# PPC
SEP 1 10 41 AM '77

Citizens Committee For Action

CLEAN GOVERNMENT                           FAIR LAWS
FAIRFIELD, CALIFORNIA


VOTERS!!!!!     DON'T BE INTIMIDATED!!!!!


     On November 8, 1977, the City of Fairfield will hold a
Special Election to fill the vacant seat for Mayor. If you believe in the last voice of the people - the secret ballot - you
will read the section of the State Election Code that follows.
The State legislators have provided every voter the right to
make his or her own private choice of candidates or measure on
the ballot. NO ONE - friend, employer or relative - has the right
to dictate your VOTE!

     Article 2, Section 29620 of California Election Code:
CORRUPTION OF VOTERS: "A person shall not directly or
through another person give, offer, or promise any office, place,
or employment to procure or endeavor to procure any office, place
or employment to or for any voter, or to or for any other person,
in order to induce that voter at any election to:
     (a) Refrain from voting
     (b) Vote for any particular person
     (c) Refrain from voting for any particular person
A violation of any of the provision of this section shall be
punishable by imprisonment in the State Prison for 16 months or
two or three years."

     Article 3, Section 29630 of California Election Code:
INTIMIDATION OF VOTERS: "Every person who makes use of or
threatens to make use of any force, violence or tactic of coercion
or intimidation, to induce or compel any other person to vote or
refrain from voting at any election or to vote or refrain from
voting for any particular person at any election, or because any
person voted or refrained from voting at any election or voted or
refrained from voting for any particular person at any election is
punishable by imprisonment in the State Prison for 16 months or
two or three years."

     Article 3, Section 29643 of California Election Code:
SECRECY OF BALLOT: "Every member of a precinct board is
guilty of a misdemeanor who, prior to putting the ballot of a voter
in the ballot box, commits any of the following:
     (a) Attempts to find out the name on the ballot
     (b) Opens or suffers to be opened or examined, the
folded ballot of any voter which has been handed in
     (c) Makes or places any mark or device on any folded
ballot with a view to ascertaining the name of any person for whom
the voter has voted"

sections.question_synthetic: What disclosure, if any, would be necessary regarding the cost of printing a flyer that the Citizens Committee for Action intends to disseminate?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide a complete response without additional information and requests the requester contact them to discuss the matter further.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is partially illegible with OCR errors. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter indicates this is an incomplete response pending further information from the requester. The attached flyer content regarding voter intimidation and election code sections appears to be the subject matter of the inquiry.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What disclosure, if any, would be necessary regarding the cost of printing a flyer that the Citizens Committee for Action intends to disseminate?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide a complete response without additional information and requests the requester contact them to discuss the matter further.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Mrs. Affa M. Hawkes' inquiry about disclosure requirements for printing costs of a flyer to be distributed by the Citizens Committee for Action. The FPPC requests additional information before providing guidance.
